questions.question: What are the three fundamental stages of the CPU instruction cycle, in their correct sequential order?
options: Fetch, Decode, Execute
Decode, Fetch, Execute
Execute, Fetch, Decode
Fetch, Write-back, Decode
Load, Store, Process

questions.question: In digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of Static Timing Analysis (STA)?
options: To verify the functional correctness of the design by simulating all possible input combinations.
To calculate and verify that all timing requirements (e.g., clock frequency, setup, hold times) are met without requiring test vectors.
To synthesize the RTL code into a gate-level netlist.
To optimize the power consumption of the circuit by identifying leakage paths.
To generate the physical layout (mask data) of the integrated circuit.

questions.question: When data is written to a cache, how the update is propagated to main memory is determined by the cache's write policy. Which policy immediately writes data to both the cache and main memory upon a write operation?
options: Write-back
Write-through
Write-allocate
No-write-allocate
Least Recently Used (LRU)

questions.question: In advanced digital IC verification, what is 'Formal Verification' primarily used for, as distinct from simulation?
options: To simulate the design with a comprehensive set of test vectors to check functionality.
To mathematically prove or disprove the correctness of a design with respect to a given specification, covering all possible input sequences and states.
To verify the physical layout rules (DRC) and electrical connectivity (LVS).
To measure the power consumption of the circuit under various operating conditions.
To generate the test patterns for manufacturing defect testing.

questions.question: What is the primary motivation behind the growing adoption of 'chiplet' architectures in modern high-performance computing and SoC designs?
options: To reduce the overall power consumption by using smaller, less complex dies.
To enable the integration of diverse functionalities (e.g., CPU, GPU, memory controller, I/O) manufactured on different process technologies into a single package, improving yield and scalability.
To simplify the design verification process by breaking down the system into smaller, independently verifiable units.
To eliminate the need for inter-die communication protocols.
To primarily improve the operating frequency of the individual processing cores.
